ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 / Wicked Panda web-shell post-exploitation: IIS w3wp spawning recon shells (China Chopper / ANTSWORD pattern)
ucs.description: Hunts hands-on-keyboard activity dropped through APT41's signature web-shell deployment after exploiting internet-facing applications (Citrix NetScaler, ProxyLogon, IIS, Confluence, Telerik, GovWin / state-government 2021 intrusions). Web shells like CHINACHOPPER, ANTSWORD, and the BARIUM/Wicked Panda custom variants force IIS w3wp.exe (or Tomcat/Java) to spawn cmd.exe/powershell.exe and run quick recon (whoami / net / nltest / quser / systeminfo) — a behaviour APT41 has been documented using repeatedly by Mandiant (M-Trends 2022, US state govts) and Microsoft (BARIUM).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","jenkins.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","route.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eventstats dc(process_name) as distinct_children by dest user parent_process_name | where distinct_children >= 2 OR process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","rundll32.exe") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 / BARIUM web-shell child-process recon — Sentinel SecurityEvent (Windows 4688)
let _suspect_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","route.exe"]);
let _web_parents = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","jenkins.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend ChildBin  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentBin in (_web_parents)
| where ChildBin  in (_suspect_children)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, AccountDomain, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 Web Shell Child Process (IIS / Tomcat -> Recon Shell)
id: 2c5e7a3a-6d1c-4d7c-9a0a-apt41-webshell-001
status: experimental
description: Detects APT41 / BARIUM / Wicked Panda hands-on-keyboard recon executed through web shells (CHINACHOPPER, ANTSWORD) dropped after exploitation of internet-facing applications.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-us-state-governments
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-05
tags:
  - attack.initial-access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1087.001
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\jenkins.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\route.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IIS/Tomcat health-check scripts that shell out to whoami / hostname (rare, baseline first)
  - Application-deployment automation that uses bitsadmin/certutil from inside a web app pool
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT41's go-to access vector across the past 5 years has been exploitation of internet-facing apps (Citrix NetScaler, ProxyLogon, Confluence, Log4Shell, Cisco IOS, US state-gov GovWin). Mandiant and Microsoft have repeatedly documented that within minutes of a successful exploit, the operator drops a CHINACHOPPER / ANTSWORD web shell onto the IIS or Tomcat server and immediately runs whoami, net gr
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 / Brass Typhoon DUSTPAN / DUSTTRAP loader: signed-vendor binary executing from non-install paths (DLL-side-load relocation)
ucs.description: Hunts the signed-binary-relocation pattern Mandiant attributes to APT41's DUSTPAN / DUSTTRAP / DEADEYE loader chain (M-Trends 2024) and that Microsoft tracks under Brass Typhoon: a legitimately-signed vendor binary (Microsoft, McAfee, Symantec, Cisco, Citrix, NVIDIA, Adobe, Google, VMware) is dropped into ProgramData / Public / AppData with a malicious sibling DLL it then side-loads. The loader is genuine and signed, but its execution path is not its installation path — a single high-fidelity signal that has burned multiple APT41 intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*") AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Program Files\\*" AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Program Files (x86)\\*" AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Windows\\System32\\*" AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.process_hash Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=left process_hash [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process_signer) as signer values(Processes.process_company) as company from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` ] | where (match(company,"(?i)microsoft|mcafee|symantec|trend\s*micro|kaspersky|cisco|vmware|citrix|fortinet|forticlient|adobe|google|nvidia|avast|eset")) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT41 / Brass Typhoon DUSTPAN/DUSTTRAP signed-loader relocation hunt
let _suspect_paths = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\$Recycle.Bin\"]);
let _vendor_signers = dynamic(["Microsoft","McAfee","Symantec","Trend Micro","Kaspersky","Cisco","VMware","Citrix","Fortinet","Adobe","Google","NVIDIA","Avast","ESET","Hewlett-Packard","Lenovo"]);
// Step 1: signed legit-vendor binary running from a writable, non-install path
let SuspectLoaders =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where isnotempty(ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName)
    | where ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_vendor_signers)
    | where FolderPath has_any (_suspect_paths)
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)"
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32"
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64"
    | where FolderPath !has @"\Microsoft\EdgeUpdate\"
    | where FolderPath !has @"\Microsoft\Teams\"
    | where FolderPath !has @"\GoogleUpdate\"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, ProcessId,
              Signer = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              OriginalName = ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName,
              ProductName = ProcessVersionInfoProductName,
              ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
// Step 2: confirm a sibling DLL was loaded out of the same folder (the side-load payload)
SuspectLoaders
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where FolderPath has_any (_suspect_paths)
    | project DllTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId,
              SideloadedDll = FileName, SideloadFolder = FolderPath, SideloadSHA256 = SHA256
  ) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where SideloadFolder =~ FolderPath and SideloadSHA256 != SHA256
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Signer, OriginalName, ProductName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine, SideloadedDll, SideloadSHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 / Brass Typhoon DUSTPAN/DUSTTRAP — signed-vendor binary executing from non-install path (Sentinel ASIM)
let _suspect_paths = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\$Recycle.Bin\"]);
let _vendor_signers = dynamic(["Microsoft","McAfee","Symantec","Trend Micro","Kaspersky","Cisco","VMware","Citrix","Fortinet","Adobe","Google","NVIDIA","Avast","ESET","Hewlett-Packard","Lenovo"]);
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where isnotempty(TargetProcessFileCompany)
| where TargetProcessFileCompany has_any (_vendor_signers)
| extend TargetPathLower = tolower(TargetProcessName)
| where TargetPathLower has_any (_suspect_paths)
| where TargetPathLower !contains @"\program files\"
| where TargetPathLower !contains @"\program files (x86)\"
| where TargetPathLower !contains @"\windows\system32\"
| where TargetPathLower !contains @"\windows\syswow64\"
| where TargetPathLower !contains @"\microsoft\edgeupdate\"
| where TargetPathLower !contains @"\microsoft\teams\"
| where TargetPathLower !contains @"\googleupdate\"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          TargetProcessName, TargetProcessFileCompany,
          TargetProcessFileDescription, TargetProcessFileOriginalName,
          TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Signed-binary side-load relocation is APT41's most consistent post-exploitation tradecraft across a decade of operations: Winnti's ASUS Live Update / NetSarang Xshell / CCleaner supply-chain implants used the same pattern, the 2022 US state-government intrusions Mandiant published (LOWKEY) used DEADEYE side-load, and DUSTPAN/DUSTTRAP (M-Trends 2024) explicitly drop an AV-vendor-signed loader (Syma

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `TargetProcessFileOriginalName` is not a canonical column of ImProcessCreate. Did you mean `ActingProcessFileOriginalName`?
